enforced.mr_name: Flag bit 0x4 exclusivity violation on mapped read
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject a SAM record where FLAG 0x4 (segment unmapped) is set but RNAME is not '*' and POS is not 0. The error-consensus oracle tags REJECT or CRASH; silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.
Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject a SAM record where the sum of query-consuming CIGAR operations (M, I, S, =, X) does not equal len(SEQ). The error-consensus oracle tags REJECT or CRASH; silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.
Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

enforced.mr_name: TLEN sign consistency violation for paired reads
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject or flag a SAM record where both reads of a paired template carry TLEN values with the same sign (both positive or both negative), violating the spec rule that the two ends must have differing signs. The error-consensus oracle tags REJECT or CRASH; silent acceptance is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: The TLEN field is positive for the leftmost segment of the template, negative for the rightmost, and the sign for any middle segment is undefined. If segments cover the same coordinates then the choice of which is leftmost and rightmost is arbitrary, but the two ends must still have differing signs.

The TLEN field is positive for the leftmost segment of the template, negative for the rightmost, and the sign for any middle segment is undefined. If segments cover the same coordinates then the choice of which is leftmost and rightmost is arbitrary, but the two ends must still have differing signs.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM alignment optional tag field order invariance
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the order of optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields on each alignment line, the parsed data must be semantically identical to the original. The order of optional fields is not significant per the SAM spec.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

All optional fields follow the TAG:TYPE:VALUE format where TAG is a two-character string that matches /[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]/. Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @HD header line subtag order invariance
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within the @HD header line, the parsed header must be semantically identical. The order of fields within a header line is not significant.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @SQ header line subtag order invariance
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within each @SQ header line, the parsed header must be semantically identical. The order of fields within a header line is not significant.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @RG header line subtag order invariance
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within each @RG header line, the parsed header must be semantically identical. The order of fields within a header line is not significant.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @PG header line subtag order invariance
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within each @PG header line, the parsed header must be semantically identical. The order of fields within a header line is not significant.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the order of @CO comment lines in the header, the parsed data must be semantically identical. @CO lines are free-text comments with no ordering semantics.

evidence.quote: In the header, each line is TAB-delimited and, apart from @CO lines, each data field follows a format `TAG:VALUE' where TAG is a two-character string that defines the format and content of VALUE. Thus header lines match /^@(HD|SQ|RG|PG)(\t[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]:[ -~]+)+$/ or /^@CO\t.*/.

ambiguity_flags: @CO lines are explicitly excluded from the TAG:VALUE format, indicating they are free-form text with no structured ordering semantics.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header record type reordering invariance (@SQ, @RG, @PG)
quarantine.oracle: After reordering @SQ, @RG, and @PG header records among themselves (while keeping @HD first), the parsed data must be semantically identical. The spec does not impose ordering among these record types beyond @HD being first.

evidence.quote: @HD & File-level metadata. Optional. If present, there must be only one @HD line and it must be the first line of the file.

ambiguity_flags: The spec only mandates @HD be first; no explicit statement is made about the relative order of @SQ, @RG, @PG records, but @SQ order defines coordinate sort key order.

quarantine.oracle: After splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR operations (e.g., 10M â‡Œ 4M6M), the total query-consuming length and reference-consuming length must remain unchanged, and the biological interpretation of the alignment must be identical.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 â€” Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should' (advisory), not 'must' â€” a conformant parser may accept adjacent identical CIGAR ops, but the normalization MR tests that splitting/merging them preserves semantics

quarantine.mr_name: Illegal optional tag type character rejection
quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject a SAM record where an optional tag's type character is not one of the valid types (A, i, f, Z, H, B). The error-consensus oracle tags REJECT or CRASH; silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: As with alignment optional fields (see Section~sec:alnaux), you can freely add new tags for further data fields.

ambiguity_flags: The SAMtags specification (Section sec:alnaux) defines valid type characters as A, i, f, Z, H, B, but the exact normative statement about illegal characters was not retrieved below threshold. The rule is well-established in the SAMtags spec that type characters outside this set are invalid.

quarantine.mr_name: POS=0 unmapped read invariant after sut_write_roundtrip
quarantine.oracle: For any alignment record where FLAG has bit 0x4 set and RNAME is '*', POS must remain 0 after write-roundtrip; the writer must not change POS from 0 to any other value or vice versa

evidence.quote: SAM stands for Sequence Alignment/Map format. It is a TAB-delimited text format consisting of a header section, which is optional, and an alignment section.

ambiguity_flags: SAM spec allows unmapped segments to have ordinary coordinates for sorting purposes, so POS=0 is only required when RNAME='*' and no coordinate is available

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header subtag order invariance (HD, SQ, RG, PG)
preconditions: has_hd_line or has_sq_line or has_rg_line or has_pg_line
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs within each header line, re-parsing and re-serializing must yield the same canonical content (with sorted subtags) as the original. The spec states that within each (non-@CO) header line, the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.
